notes: Flat-rate IT asset disposal service: $49/unit recycling or $69/unit value recovery; evolved into Dell Technologies Asset Recovery Services
Dell's first official reuse/recycling program launched in early 1990s; predecessor to ARS
Military-grade data wiping standard referenced for high-security disk sanitization; largely superseded by NIST SP 800-88
Multi-pass sector overwriting for data destruction; still used but supplemented by degaussing and physical destruction for SSDs
Referenced as typical 36-month-old PC processor; launched 1999
Referenced as bulky hazardous e-waste requiring special handling; fully replaced by LCD/LED displays
8-GB and 20-GB models cited for data wiping time benchmarks; product line continued but capacities now in TB range
End-of-life process framework including valuation, cleansing, packing, recycling, sales, and verification
Component-level disassembly for material recovery from electronic devices; now a major global industry
